channel.title: Decarbonarma
channel.description: Clear, no-nonsense analysis of UK home decarbonisation - heat pumps, solar, EVs, batteries and the grid.

item.title: Where Do You Start? The Two Front Doors Into a Low-Carbon Home
item.description: Most people begin their low-carbon journey one of two ways: with solar, or with an EV. An interactive map of both routes — and how replacing your roof or your car decides the first step.

item.title: Putin's Accidental Climate Policy
item.description: How the invasion of Ukraine and the collapse of Russian gas drove Europe's clean energy transition faster than three decades of climate policy.

item.title: 2,700 Heat Deaths — Why Cooling Heat Pumps Matter
item.description: The official Met Office/Imperial toll from this summer's heatwaves passed 2,700. Why most heat pumps can't cool well, and what worked at Rose Cottage.

item.title: Solar Becomes Law in 2027 — Does Resale Value Survive?
item.description: From March 2027 every new English home needs solar covering 40% of its footprint. Once it's law, not a luxury, does the resale premium survive?

item.title: Britain's Grid Keeps Getting Margin Notices
item.description: Three heatwave-driven Electricity Margin Notices in one summer, low wind, and French interconnectors that couldn't help. Why supply security matters.

item.title: Has the British Summer Started Needing Air-Con?
item.description: We counted every day above 25C at Heathrow for five years. British summers are turning into an air-con season — a heat pump that cools on solar is the answer.

item.title: EV Charger + Solar + Battery: The CT-Clamp Trick
item.description: Add an EV charger to a home with solar and a battery and the two can fight each other. The fix: a Henley block and getting your CT clamps in the right order.

item.title: UK Wind: Status H1 2026
item.description: Where UK wind power stands in mid-2026 — capacity and output, what's under construction, the record AR7 auction, floating wind, and curtailment costs.

item.title: Which EV Charger Should You Buy?
item.description: Don't buy the wrong EV charger. A plain-English decision flow from 'should I go electric?' to exactly which home charging setup fits your driveway and tariff.

item.title: The Beginner's Guide to Air Source Heat Pumps
item.description: Ground source, air-to-water, air-to-air — three different machines all called 'heat pumps'. Which is simplest, and which one is basically air-con.

item.title: Daikin X Series vs Samsung EHS Quint: Worth It?
item.description: Daikin's X Series and Samsung's EHS Quint both blend air-to-air and air-to-water heating in one unit. What's real, what's marketing, what buyers need to know.

item.title: Nuclear Power in the UK: Where It Stands in 2026
item.description: Britain's ageing reactors are winding down as a new generation of giant plants and small modular reactors gears up. The full 2026 picture, with charts.

item.title: 3 Mad EV Lease Deals Right Now
item.description: Three genuinely cheap UK EV leases from £137/month — plus what the brochures skip: real running costs once you charge overnight or off solar.

item.title: H1 2026: Six Months of Solar & Battery Data
item.description: Six months, four solar arrays, one battery: the full metered H1 2026 breakdown from Rose Cottage, plus battery cycling and the real financial return.

item.title: Air-to-Water Heat Pumps Are Dead
item.description: Air-to-water heat pumps dominate UK grants, but air-to-air is cheaper, simpler and works year-round. The case for a quiet revolution, and the honest caveats.

item.title: My A2A Heat Pump + Sunamp Setup, Not a Gas Boiler
item.description: Why I chose an air-to-air heat pump and a Sunamp heat battery over a gas boiler or air-to-water system — real costs, the grant catch, who it suits.

item.title: The Top 5 Home EV Chargers (2026)
item.description: The five best home EV chargers for 2026, ranked on tech and integration with Intelligent Octopus Go, the cheap-charging tariff. The good, bad and ugly of each.

item.title: How to Buy, Size and Run a Gas Boiler Efficiently
item.description: Heat pumps are the right long-term answer, but if gas is still your only option: how to size, buy and run a boiler to use the least gas possible.

item.title: The Cheapest New Car in Britain?
item.description: A new electric SUV is leasing for £138.55 a month, cheaper than a city car. What you really pay after tax, insurance and charging, and whether it's any good.

item.title: World Decarbonisation: State of Play, H1 2026
item.description: Renewables overtook coal globally in 2025. A review of the world's biggest decarbonisation efforts from January to May 2026, and what comes next.

item.title: Rose Cottage: From Gas to Nearly Zero
item.description: How one homeowner combined fabric upgrades, a heating cascade, solar PV and smart energy management to cut gas use by 60% in a season, with the data.

item.title: The Netherlands is Running Out of Grid. The UK is Next
item.description: 7,300 Dutch households are stuck in a decade-long grid connection queue. What the Netherlands' electrification crunch means for Britain's own grid plans.

item.title: Nature as Climate Engine
item.description: Reintroducing a single predator can reshape a landscape and pull carbon back into the soil — how trophic cascades make nature a powerful climate tool.

item.title: Home Energy Optimiser
item.description: A free tool that works out the cheapest way to run your home's actual heating mix, picks the right tariff, and ranks every upgrade by payback period.

item.title: 5 Home Battery Storage Systems Worth Considering
item.description: Five UK home battery storage systems compared cheapest to premium, with real specs and prices, from budget DIY kits to fully managed smart systems.

item.title: Does Brand Matter? What 600+ Heat Pumps Tell Us
item.description: Vaillant vs Mitsubishi vs Samsung vs Daikin: what 639 real, metered heat pumps from HeatpumpMonitor.org actually say about whether brand affects performance.

item.title: Eddi + Sunamp + Tepeo ZEB: The Triple Barrel
item.description: Adding a Tepeo Zero Emission Boiler to an Eddi-and-Sunamp setup pushes Scotland's curtailed wind absorption even higher. Part 2 of the Wasted Wind series.

item.title: The Real Cost of Going Electric: EV vs Diesel
item.description: A diesel Mercedes GLE and Mini against their electric equivalents, same household, same mileage. The real cost comparison, with no fudged assumptions.

item.title: Can an EV Pay for Itself? The Frontera Test
item.description: Can switching from petrol to a £138.55-a-month EV lease pay for itself in saved fuel, tax, MOT and tyres? We ran the numbers on the Vauxhall Frontera.

item.title: Decarbonised Your Home. Should You Have Bought Stocks?
item.description: £30,000 on solar, a battery and a heat pump is a bet on the green economy. We compare that against what the same money would've returned in green stocks.

item.title: Is a Heat Pump Worth It in the UK? 2026 Answer
item.description: No sales pitch. A homeowner who fitted his own heat pump runs the real 2026 numbers — grant, running costs vs gas, and when it genuinely isn't worth it.

item.title: SCOP 6 Has Already Been Broken
item.description: A six-year-old Austrian startup, Lambda Wärmepumpen, has posted a WPZ-certified SCOP of 6.06, breaking a barrier the industry wasn't expecting yet.

item.title: HeatpumpMonitor.org — What the Data Actually Says
item.description: What 639 real UK heat pumps, tracked by HeatpumpMonitor.org, reveal about efficiency, flow temperatures and what good installation design actually looks like.

item.title: COP 4.8 in Scotland: Barry Sharp's NIBE S1155 PVT
item.description: A NIBE S1155 PVT ground source heat pump using roof-mounted PVT panels instead of a borehole has delivered a COP of 4.8 in Scotland over 954 days.

item.title: Drill, Baby, Drill? UK Domestic Fossil Fuels
item.description: New North Sea licences and fracking are now banned in the UK. The case for and against domestic fossil fuel production, and why the tension remains.

item.title: Samsung EHS Quint Deep Dive: One Box, Five Modes
item.description: A technical breakdown of Samsung's EHS Quint — the first heat pump doing air-to-air and air-to-water from one unit, with free hot water from cooling.

item.title: Can SMRs Replace Britain's Ageing Gas Fleet?
item.description: The UK signed its first Small Modular Reactor contract in 2026. Replacing the whole gas fleet would need roughly 70 reactors and £175 billion.

item.title: Are Solar Panels Worth It in the UK in 2026?
item.description: No sales pitch. The real 2026 cost of solar and a battery, honest payback by region, and when it's not worth it — from a homeowner who fitted it.

item.title: Cheap or Premium Solar? The Real Differences
item.description: Budget panels vs premium panels, microinverters, and the battery brands everyone argues about: the real quality differences that matter in a solar system.

item.title: Shell Bought Home Battery Storage, Then Shelved It
item.description: Shell paid around €500 million for home battery startup Sonnen in 2019, then tried to sell it five years later. What went wrong with the Green Acquisition.

item.title: The Heat Battery That Replaced Our Hot Water Tank
item.description: When Rose Cottage's hot water cylinder failed, a Sunamp Thermino 300e replaced it in four hours and has run on free solar surplus ever since. How it works.

item.title: Superfoil + Solar Roof Retrofit: What It Cost
item.description: A re-roof with Superfoil insulation and solar gets the whole-home energy bill to £20/month — real cost, metered output, is multifoil worth it?

item.title: Can the Grid Handle Electrifying Every UK Home?
item.description: 23 million gas boilers, 37 million EVs, 100% clean power by 2030: we ran the maths on whether the UK grid can actually handle full electrification.

item.title: The Wind's Blowing, Turbines Are Off — You're Paying
item.description: Britain pays wind farms to switch off while firing up gas elsewhere to cover the gap, costing every household £34 in 2025. The scandal, and the fix.

item.title: Why Home Wind Power Doesn't Add Up
item.description: A turbine on your roof seems like the natural twin to solar panels. The cube law explains why home wind almost never works, in plain English.
